description: xrpl wallet fund — Fund a testnet or devnet address from the faucet via a single CLI command

userStories.title: wallet fund <address> — fund address from testnet/devnet faucet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to fund a testnet address from the faucet so I can test transactions without visiting a web UI.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl wallet fund <address-or-alias> POSTs to the correct faucet URL based on the active network: testnet node → https://faucet.altnet.rippletest.net/accounts, devnet node → https://faucet.devnet.rippletest.net/accounts

Network detection: if resolved WSS URL contains 'altnet' use testnet faucet; if contains 'devnet' use devnet faucet; any other URL exits 1 with: 'Error: wallet fund is only available on testnet and devnet'
POST body is JSON: { "destination": "<address>" }; uses Node 22 built-in fetch (no extra HTTP lib)
After faucet POST succeeds, polls account_info on the XRPL node until account appears: max 10 retries with 2000ms delay between each
Resolves alias to address via resolveAccount() using getKeystoreDir({ keystore: undefined })
On success prints two lines: 'Funded <address>' and 'Balance: <amount> XRP'
Exits 1 with faucet error message to stderr if POST returns non-2xx status
Exits 1 with 'Error: account did not appear on ledger after 10 retries' if polling times out
Uses getNodeUrl(cmd) for node resolution — no local --node flag on this command
E2E test: generate a fresh wallet address with wallet new --json, run wallet fund <address> --node testnet, assert exit 0 and stdout contains 'Funded' and 'Balance:'
E2E test: run wallet fund <address> with --node wss://xrplcluster.com (mainnet), assert exit 1 and stderr contains 'only available on testnet and devnet'
E2E test: --json output contains address, balanceXrp, and balanceDrops fields with non-zero values
